title: El Mayo Sentencing Postponed AGAIN — Rescheduled to May 18 as Defense Cites Mexico Violence
detail: For the second time, the sentencing hearing for Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada García has been postponed — now rescheduled from April 13, 2026 to May 18, 2026 in Brooklyn federal court. Defense lawyer Frank Pérez filed the postponement request citing violence and instability inside Mexico that has made it difficult to gather necessary case documentation and witness information. Judge Brian Cogan approved the delay. El Mayo, 77, faces mandatory life imprisonment plus a $15 billion forfeiture judgment after pleading guilty in August 2025 to leading a continuing criminal enterprise (RICO) and drug trafficking conspiracy spanning decades. The postponement marks the second delay in the highest-profile US drug trafficking sentencing in history — the January 13, 2026 hearing was delayed at defense request, and now the April 13 date has also slipped. The sentencing, whenever it occurs, will formally close the legal chapter on El Mayo's 2024 arrest — the event that fractured the Sinaloa Cartel and triggered the ongoing civil war between Los Chapitos and Los Mayos that has killed hundreds and displaced tens of thousands in Sinaloa.

media.caption: El Mayo Sentencing Postponed AGAIN — Rescheduled to May 18 as Defense Cites Mexico Violence

title: Sinaloa Cartel-Linked Duo Sentenced in Miami for 2022 Execution at Airport Hotel
detail: Tsvia Kol, 37, of Hallandale Beach, Florida, and Jimmy Sanchez, 38, of Spring Valley, California, received lengthy federal prison sentences on April 10, 2026 for a Sinaloa Cartel-directed execution that occurred in 2022 at a hotel near Miami International Airport. The duo was convicted of using a firearm to cause death and conspiracy to distribute over 500 grams of methamphetamine. The victim, Julio Gonzalez, 46, was killed execution-style at the Aladdin Hotel in Miami Springs. The case illustrates the Sinaloa Cartel's operational reach into the continental United States — using local criminal networks for targeted violence — and comes days after El Mayo Zambada's sentencing was again postponed.

media.caption: Sinaloa Cartel-Linked Duo Sentenced in Miami for 2022 Execution at Airport Hotel
